title: Reflexive Verbs (Reflexivos y Se)
description: Plain-English lesson on reflexive verbs: subject = object, the reflexive pronouns me/te/se/nos/se, pronoun placement, and common daily-routine verbs.

lessons.summary: A reflexive verb is one where the subject and the object are the same — the do-er and the receiver are one person (levantar = to raise; levantarse = to raise oneself = to get up). They need reflexive pronouns: me, te, se, nos, se. The pronoun normally goes before the conjugated verb: me llamo, te lavas, se levanta, nos duchamos, se despiertan. After another verb it can attach to the infinitive: quiere peinarse. Careful: me/te/se/nos also work as other kinds of pronoun, so read the sentence before deciding.

examples.english: What time did you get up?

examples.spanish: Él se lava los pies con agua caliente.
examples.english: He washes his feet with hot water.
She wants to comb her hair.

items.explanation: The reflexive pronoun for él/ella/usted is 'se'.

items.promptEnglish: What are you called? — I'm called Ana.

items.prompt: Yo ___ a las seis cada día. (levantarse — 'I get up')
items.promptEnglish: I get up at six every day.
items.explanation: Reflexive pronoun 'me' before the conjugated verb 'levanto'.

items.prompt: Ella ___ las manos con jabón. (lavarse — 'she washes')
items.promptEnglish: She washes her hands with soap.
items.explanation: Reflexive pronoun 'se' before 'lava'. (Spanish uses the reflexive, not a possessive, for body parts.)

items.promptEnglish: She wants to comb her hair.
items.explanation: After another verb the reflexive infinitive keeps -se attached: quiere peinarse (also valid: se quiere peinar).

items.prompt: Make it reflexive: "Levanto a las seis" → "___ a las seis" (I get up at six).
items.explanation: levantarse is reflexive; for 'yo' add 'me' before the verb.

items.prompt: Make it negative: "Se levantó temprano" → "___" (He didn't get up early).
items.explanation: 'No' goes before the reflexive pronoun: no se levantó.

items.prompt: Describe tu rutina de la mañana usando dos verbos reflexivos.
items.promptEnglish: Describe your morning routine using two reflexive verbs.
items.guidance: Must be grammatical Spanish with at least two reflexive verbs, each with the correct reflexive pronoun before the conjugated verb (e.g. 'Me levanto a las siete y me ducho'). Mark incorrect if reflexive pronouns are missing or mismatched to the subject.
items.sampleAnswer: Me despierto a las seis, me levanto y me lavo la cara.
items.explanation: Reflexive verbs need a reflexive pronoun (me/te/se/nos) agreeing with the subject.
